differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Structured data in tables with predefined schemas and relationships.
differences.rightDesc: Flexible schemas, supports documents, key-value, graph, or column-family.

differences.title: 2. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Scale up by adding more power to a single server.
differences.rightDesc: Scale out by adding more servers to a cluster.

differences.title: 3. QUERY LANGUAGE
differences.leftDesc: Standardized Structured Query Language (SQL) for complex queries.
differences.rightDesc: Each NoSQL type has its own query methods or APIs.

differences.title: 4. SCHEMA MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Requires upfront schema definition; changes can be complex.
differences.rightDesc: Schemas can evolve; easily adapt to changing data requirements.

differences.title: 5. ACID COMPLIANCE
differences.leftDesc: Ensures Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability for transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Often prioritizes availability and partition tolerance over immediate consistency.

differences.title: 6. JOIN OPERATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Efficiently handles complex joins across multiple tables.
differences.rightDesc: Joins are typically limited or not supported; data often denormalized.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Best for financial systems, ERPs, where data integrity is key.
differences.rightDesc: Ideal for big data, real-time apps, content management, IoT.
